<!-- Generated by StrongAIAutoDoc 20260524 -->

This directory defines the shared schemas, defaults, and interchange formats that power GuardDog architecture reviews and its CLI. It standardizes configuration resolution (including GitHub issue behavior and token budgets), the canonical structure of review findings and final results, and the metadata used to rank and select repository files for an LLM context window. Together, these modules provide stable, validated contracts between the review runner, ranking/selection logic, and downstream output and integrations.

```mermaid
C4Component
title GuardDog Shared Schemas & Contracts (TypeScript)

System_Boundary(dir, "Directory: shared types & schemas") {
  Component(config, "config.ts", "TS module", "Configuration schema, defaults, CLI option types; GitHub issue settings; token budgets")
  Component(finding, "finding.ts", "TS module", "Finding and review-result types; severity/impact scales; strict JSON Schemas")
  Component(contextManifest, "contextManifest.ts", "TS module", "Context selection manifest shapes; skip reasons; token-usage accounting")
  Component(contextRank, "contextRank.ts", "TS module", "Context ranking result types; strict JSON Schema; safe defaults")
  Component(repoMap, "repoMap.ts", "TS module", "Repository map schema; packages and notable paths; optional context selection metadata")
}

System_Ext(cli, "GuardDog Review CLI", "External system", "Parses options, resolves config, orchestrates review")
System_Ext(reviewEngine, "Review Runner / LLM Orchestrator", "External system", "Builds prompts, consumes schemas, produces results")
System_Ext(ranker, "Context Ranker (LLM/Heuristic)", "External system", "Generates ordered file paths for context inclusion")
System_Ext(fs, "Repository Filesystem", "External system", "Source tree scanned to produce repo maps and context candidates")
System_Ext(github, "GitHub Issues API", "External system", "Optional publishing of findings as issues")

Rel(config, finding, "Imports severity/impact types for threshold fields", "Type import")
Rel(repoMap, contextManifest, "Imports context-selection metadata shape", "Type import")

Rel(cli, config, "Reads CLI options, resolves defaults, validates configuration", "Uses")
Rel(cli, finding, "Serializes/deserializes findings and review results", "Uses")
Rel(cli, github, "Creates issues per configured mode and labels", "Calls")

Rel(reviewEngine, finding, "Produces structured review result constrained by schema", "Uses")
Rel(reviewEngine, repoMap, "Consumes repo map to build factual repo context", "Uses")
Rel(reviewEngine, contextManifest, "Records and emits why files were included/skipped", "Uses")
Rel(ranker, contextRank, "Emits rankedPaths payload validated by schema", "Uses")

Rel(reviewEngine, fs, "Reads files and metadata for mapping and context building", "Reads")
Rel(cli, fs, "Reads config/design files; writes outputs", "Reads/Writes")
```

Key components: config.ts centralizes resolved configuration and CLI option types, including token budgets and GitHub issue modes. finding.ts defines the canonical finding/review-result contracts plus strict JSON Schemas that enable constrained LLM output and consistent downstream processing. contextRank.ts and contextManifest.ts work together: a ranker emits a validated ordering (rankedPaths), then selection logic records inclusion decisions, skip reasons, and token usage in a manifest for auditability. repoMap.ts captures a factual repository summary and optionally embeds contextSelection metadata, linking filesystem scanning to prompt assembly and reporting.
